headline: EV Charging Stations Cover Only 2,800 of 10,000 Needed
context: Malaysia has only 2,800 public EV charging stations as of February 2026 against target of 10,000 needed.

cards.big: Only 2,800 EV chargers available against 10,000 target needed
cards.sub: EV sales grew 180% in 2025 but charging infrastructure lagged behind

cards.big: 82,000 EVs registered but only 1 charger per 29 vehicles nationally
cards.sub: International benchmark is 1 charger per 10 EVs for adequate coverage

cards.big: 85% of stations concentrated in KL Selangor and Penang
cards.sub: East coast and East Malaysia have fewer than 120 charging points combined

cards.big: TNB committed RM500 million for 5,000 chargers but deployed only 1,800
cards.sub: Grid capacity constraints limit home charging installation options

cards.big: Charging gaps test whether EV policy addresses infrastructure before adoption
2,800 of 10,000 EV chargers installed. Adoption follows infrastructure, and the infrastructure is 72% short.
